competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: You
competitors.rules: Your real Robinhood account, rebased to the shared origin so it's comparable. Performance only — holdings and trades are deliberately not published.

competitors.name: congress_mirror
competitors.rules: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: Robinhood
competitors.rules: Real Robinhood Agentic cash account (••••) running the funded algorithms (100% momentum_breakout) live: each run-robin reruns their signals on the latest bar and trades the real account — buy fresh breakouts, exit on the strategy's sell rule.
competitors.note: Real money — the actual Robinhood Agentic account (••••), not paper. Dollar/share figures are scaled ×100 to the $10,000 display notional like the rest of the board (the live account is ~$100). Allocation: 100% momentum_breakout. Change it in state/robin_alloc.json or just ask.

competitors.name: S&P 500
competitors.rules: Buys SPY with the whole book on the first forward session and holds — the market baseline.

trade_log.reason: buy & hold: all-in on day one

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
analyst.action: hold the 7/02 rotation steady into Monday's open: exit MU before the 7/10 SKHY listing, trim extended V and TSM, add valuation-floor healthcare (REGN, GILD), upsize the two anchors (LLY into the live Medicare GLP-1 ramp, BAC into its 7/14 print)
analyst.sizing: 53% deployed across 6 names (LLY 12, BAC 12, TSM 8, V 8, REGN 8, GILD 5), 47% cash while the momentum unwind resolves

regime.label: momentum unwind inside a value/defensive rotation — no-hike relief, but no cuts either
regime.note: No session has traded since 7/02 (July 4th holiday Friday + weekend), so the tape is unchanged: Dow at a record while the SOX corrected -6.7% on the week in a memory-led unwind. Week ahead is light — Fed June-meeting minutes Wednesday 7/8 and the Q2 earnings season opener (Delta, PepsiCo) are the scheduled catalysts; Schwab's trader outlook flags 'higher volatility' with a cautious tech stance. The two dated events this book is positioned around are unchanged and confirmed over the weekend: SK Hynix's ~$29B Nasdaq ADR debut 7/10 and BAC earnings 7/14.

analyst.thesis: What changed since 7/02: nothing traded (holiday Friday, weekend), so this is a hold-steady re-queue of the same book, not a re-pick — the weekend checks all came back thesis-intact. SK Hynix confirmed the Nasdaq ADR debut is still scheduled for 7/10 (17.79M new shares, ~$29B, ticker SKHY, subject to SEC approval), so the MU full exit BEFORE the listing stands — that was the one decision I graded myself down for halving last week, and I'm finishing it. LLY's Medicare GLP-1 Bridge went live 7/1 exactly as the anchor thesis required — $50/month with prior authorization, Lilly's own release estimating ~20M Medicare patients may qualify — and the stock hit a record on 7/2; the ramp is now a live tailwind, not a promise. BAC confirmed Q2 results for Tuesday 7/14 (consensus $1.10 EPS vs $0.89 y/y) — 6 tick-days out, the dated catalyst the 12% weight was sized for. TSM guided Q2 revenue $39.0–40.2B (~+32% y/y) with the print 7/16 — the foundry leg is a fundamentals bet distinct from the memory unwind, which is why it survived the semi cut at 8% while MU goes to zero. REGN and GILD are the unfilled 7/02 limit buys re-queued at the same prices: valuation-floor healthcare (REGN ~15x forward, GILD ~17x with yield) in a tape rotating toward exactly that. Orders queue for Monday's 9:30 open; buys keep their ~1%-above-close limits so a gap-up gets skipped, exits stay market-on-open.

evidence.point: SK Hynix ADR debut confirmed on track: 'SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10' — board resolved 17.79M new shares (~KRW 45.45T ≈ $29.65B), ticker SKHY, 'tentative and subject to change' pending SEC approval

LLY Medicare GLP-1 Bridge effective July 1, 2026: Foundayo (orforglipron) and Zepbound at '$50 per month with prior authorization', running through Dec 31 2027; Lilly estimates 'roughly 20 million Medicare patients may qualify'
BAC reports Q2 2026 on Tuesday July 14 (~6:45am ET, call 8:30am); consensus EPS '$1.10 per share, up from 89 cents per share in the year-ago period', revenue est '$30.26 billion'

TSM Q2 2026 earnings conference Thursday July 16; company guidance 'revenue of $39.0–$40.2 billion (approximately 10% sequential and 32% year-over-year growth at midpoint), gross margin of 65.5–67.5%'

Week of 7/6 calendar: 'the primary economic release being the minutes from the Federal Reserve's June policy meeting scheduled for release on Wednesday' plus early Q2 reports from Delta Air Lines and PepsiCo

Last week's tape: 'chipmakers and artificial intelligence infrastructure providers faced a severe correction, with the PHLX Semiconductor Index dropping 6.7%' while the Dow set a record — the rotation this book is positioned for
7/02 settled closes (the prices this book's orders were sized against): MU 975.56, LLY 1213.91, BAC 58.73, TSM 434.16, V 362.13, REGN 654.27, GILD 131.27

risks: Rotation reverses: if semis bounce hard (an SKHY listing that prices strong could RALLY memory), exiting MU at ~975 vs my 1119.63 entry locks the -12.9% loss at the low
BAC earnings risk cuts both ways: the weak June payrolls that killed the hike also flag credit-cycle softening — a reserve-build headline on 7/14 could swamp a trading beat
LLY is extended after the record close; a Medicare Bridge ramp slower than the ~20M-eligible headline implies would deflate a rich multiple
TSM reports 7/16 into a bruised semi tape — even an in-line print can sell off when the group is de-risking
Fed minutes Wednesday could re-price the no-cuts path harsher than the dots implied; 47% cash cushions but doesn't immunize the book

data_examined.label: Robinhood daily OHLCV, 101 symbols through settled 2026-07-02
Analyst memory brief (holdings, realized P&L, alpha vs SPY, prior reflection)
SK Hynix Nasdaq ADR listing status
LLY Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program details
Week-of-7/6 macro calendar and volatility outlook

rationale.LLY: Anchor long, 12%. The dated event already resolved in my favor: the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge went live 7/1 — $50/month, prior auth, Lilly estimating ~20M eligible seniors [DATA: investor.lilly.com] — and the stock hit a record on the news [DATA: 7/2 +3.1%, tradingkey]. Consensus still treats the ramp as gradual; I think the first covered-refill data surprises fast [ASSUMED]. Top-up buy re-queued with a 1223.5 limit (~1% over the 1213.91 close) so I don't chase a gap.
rationale.BAC: Anchor long, 12%. Q2 print confirmed for Tuesday 7/14 (~6 tick-days): consensus $1.10 EPS vs $0.89 y/y on ~$30.3B revenue [DATA: stocktitan/Benzinga]. Rate-cut-less-but-no-hike is the friendliest NII backdrop banks have had all year [ASSUMED], and the name sits at a low-teens multiple while the rotation bids value [DATA: prior comp work, 7/02 report]. Top-up limit 59.28.
rationale.TSM: Hold at 8% (trim re-queued). Foundry is the semi leg whose demand is contractual, not sentiment: company-guided Q2 revenue $39.0–40.2B, ~+32% y/y, GM 65.5–67.5%, print 7/16 [DATA: investor.tsmc.com]. It fell with the group but the memory-pricing threat driving the MU exit doesn't read across to foundry [ASSUMED]. Sized as a hold, not conviction — last week's lesson.
rationale.V: Hold at 8% (trim re-queued). Entered 7/2 at 352.69, marked 362.13 (+2.7%) [DATA: analyst_memory]. Payments-network quality compounding with no dated event risk before earnings later in July; it's the book's lowest-drama sleeve [ASSUMED]. Trim keeps it from drifting above target after the pop.
rationale.REGN: New 8%, unfilled 7/02 limit buy re-queued at 660.8. Valuation-floor healthcare: ~15x forward, still ~20% off its high [DATA: 7/02 comp work], August print as the dated catalyst, in a tape rotating into exactly this profile [DATA: SOX -6.7% week vs Dow record]. The limit means a gap through 660.8 skips the entry rather than chasing.
rationale.GILD: New 5%, unfilled 7/02 limit buy re-queued at 132.55. Defensive cash-flow pharma at ~17x with a real yield [DATA: 7/02 comp work] — the small, boring ballast leg of the healthcare sleeve. Same discipline: limit or skip.
rationale.MU: Exit to zero, market-on-open — finishing the decision I halved on 7/01. The threat is dated and confirmed: SKHY's ~$29B ADR debut 7/10 at a discount multiple attacks the US-accessibility premium MU trades on [DATA: CNBC 6/24, reconfirmed this weekend]. Four tick-days out; anticipation, not confirmation. No limit on the exit — a resting sell that traps me through the listing defeats the point.

reflection.looking_back: The 7/02 tick queued the full rotation — MU exit, TSM/V trims, LLY/BAC top-ups, new REGN/GILD limit buys — but nothing has filled: Friday was the July 4th holiday and no session has traded since, so the book is unchanged at -12.7% all-time vs SPY -0.7% (alpha -12.0pts) with every order still resting for Monday's open [DATA: analyst_memory]. Per the no-new-session rule I'm grading the weekend's information, not inventing a session that didn't happen.
worked: The MU-exit thesis survived its weekend audit: SK Hynix reconfirmed the 7/10 Nasdaq debut (~$29B, SKHY) [DATA: CNBC], so exiting before the listing remains the right call and the queued order needs no adjustment

LLY's catalyst went from scheduled to live: the Medicare Bridge took effect 7/1 ($50/mo, ~20M eligible) and the stock closed 7/2 at a record [DATA: investor.lilly.com, tradingkey] — the anchor's thesis strengthened while the book slept
Queuing the buys as limits (~1% over close) cost nothing over the holiday and still protects Monday's open from a gap-up fill [DATA: pending orders in agent_state]
missed: I wrote 'BAC earnings 7/15' in the 7/02 report; the confirmed date is Tuesday 7/14 [DATA: stocktitan] — one tick-day matters when the whole book is sized around dated events
The alpha hole (-12.0pts) is unchanged because nothing traded — the fix is still unproven until the rotation orders actually fill and the events resolve
reflection.adjustment: No churn on a no-session run: re-queue the identical targets and limits rather than second-guessing resting orders, and carry the corrected BAC date (7/14) into position management — the earnings-week decision now comes one tick earlier.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.
rules.congress_mirror: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2026-01-02–2026-07-02, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-ranks its best ideas seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). The panel's rank-weighted (Borda) consensus becomes a multi-name book (top 5 names, weight ∝ points). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
